judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with comparable rigor and citation quality; Report 2 states the same-commit doc-update policy slightly more explicitly and generally, but the difference is marginal.

report_1.evidence: backend/app/api/invoices.py:4,14-16 — bypasses the gateway... backend/app/workers/dunning.py:2,9-13 — bypasses the gateway... backend/app/billing/errors.py:6 — imports chargekit.errors to re-export ChargeDeclined / ProviderUnavailable. This is the doc's acknowledged exception, and it only imports exception types (makes no provider calls), so it is benign.

report_2.evidence: backend/app/api/invoices.py — imports the raw SDK... This bypasses gateway.charge() entirely. backend/app/workers/dunning.py — imports the raw SDK... The two legitimate touchpoints are gateway.py itself (the boundary) and backend/app/billing/errors.py:6 (from chargekit import errors as ck_errors), which imports only the SDK's exception types and is the one documented exception.

report_1.evidence: InvoiceOut declares external_ref (backend/app/models/invoice.py:22), from_model populates it (:28)... Git confirms this field was added in c23da88 (MEDB-214, 2026-06-18) together with migration 0006_add_invoice_external_ref.sql

report_2.evidence: the actual serializer InvoiceOut and InvoiceOut.from_model (backend/app/models/invoice.py:15-28) return a sixth field, external_ref (declared at line 22, populated at line 28)... introduced by migrations/0006_add_invoice_external_ref.sql (ALTER TABLE invoice ADD COLUMN external_ref TEXT;)

report_1.evidence: It should be corrected, and the correction belongs with this refund work rather than deferred... In short: correct the invoice-fields and webhook lines immediately; make the gateway-reconciliation call up front and land the doc change in the same change set as the refund code — don't merge refund code while leaving the doc stale, or the drift compounds.

report_2.evidence: The discrepancies should be treated as bugs to reconcile now, before refund code is written — not documentation to "fix eventually."... On timing, the governing rule is same-commit: any change to schema, API/wire shape, route surface, or a documented invariant updates ARCHITECTURE.md in the same commit and calls it out in the PR summary.

report_1.evidence: The doc omits payment_pending and undercounts (4 vs. 5)... Stop pinning a hard count. "Handles 4 … types" couples a number to an enumeration, so it silently goes wrong the next time anyone adds a branch — which is exactly what happened. I'd restate it to point at the handler as the source of truth... and drop the fixed count, so the doc doesn't rot on the next change.

report_2.evidence: this is wrong on both count and enumeration... "4" should be "5."... My recommendation is to drop the standalone count and reference the code, since the current wording has already proven it goes stale.
